checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?

evidence.location: 10-K FY2010, Balance Sheet / Income Statement XBRL facts

checklist.question: Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?
Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?

evidence.location: 10-K FY2010 Income Statement / Cash Flow Statement

checklist.question: Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: GrossProfit=2214100000 (FY2009) / SalesRevenueNet=8256200000 (FY2009) = 26.8% margin; GrossProfit=2523000000 (FY2010) / SalesRevenueNet=9026000000 (FY2010) = 28.0% margin

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.quote: SalesRevenueNet fell from 10414500000 (FY2008) to 8256200000 (FY2009), a -20.7% decline, then rose to 9026000000 (FY2010), +9.3%; NetIncomeLoss fell from 909700000 (FY2008) to 631300000 (FY2009), -30.6%, then rose to 1029100000 (FY2010), +63.0%

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=36100000 (2008-09-30); 65000000 (2009-09-30); 99300000 (2010-09-30) -- grew faster than SalesRevenueNet growth of +9.3% FY2009 to FY2010

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: Numerous DFAN14A and SC TO-T/A filings clustered from 2010-02-05 through 2011-02-16 (e.g., DFAN14A 2010-07-29, SC TO-T/A 2010-12-22) coincide with an unsolicited tender offer/proxy contest, not accounting restatement activity; no 10-K/A or 10-Q/A filings appear in the 2007-2011 window

Periodic CORRESP/UPLOAD entries (e.g., CORRESP 2011-02-16, UPLOAD 2011-02-23, CORRESP 2011-03-28) reflect routine SEC staff correspondence, occurring alongside the tender-offer related filings rather than indicating financial statement irregularities

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Quarterly and annual XBRL data provided from FY2006/2007 through Q2 FY2011 (e.g., SalesRevenueNet start 2006-10-01 end 2007-09-30; through end 2011-03-31) across balance sheet, income statement and cash flow line items

top_signals: Allowance for doubtful accounts grew from 36.1M (FY2008) to 99.3M (FY2010), outpacing revenue growth, indicating conservative reserve building rather than earnings management
Operating cash flow consistently exceeded net income across FY2008-FY2010 (e.g., OCF=1522.4M vs NI=1029.1M in FY2010), a pattern inconsistent with aggressive accrual-based earnings inflation

Earnings volatility tracked revenue volatility through the 2008-2009 recession and 2010 recovery (NI -30.6% in FY2009, +63% in FY2010 vs revenue -20.7%/+9.3%), arguing against artificial income smoothing

Heavy 2010-2011 DFAN14A/SC TO-T/A filing volume is attributable to a hostile tender offer/proxy contest rather than accounting irregularities, and no restatement filings (10-K/A, 10-Q/A) appear in the recent period
Minor single-quarter divergence where AR grew faster than revenue (Q2 FY2011) is isolated and not part of a persistent multi-period pattern
